name: Scene Smith Heuristics
summary: How Scene Smith writes prose: honor the brief, show the world hide the gears, keep choices contrastive, respect style guardrails.

rules.statement: Every section MUST have brief_ref set to its source Section Brief ID - this is NOT optional
rules.rationale: Without brief_ref, Gatekeeper cannot find sections, Showrunner cannot track fulfillment, workflow breaks

rules.statement: Before creating a new section, search for existing sections with the same brief_ref and UPDATE rather than create new
rules.rationale: Prevents duplicate sections for the same brief

rules.statement: No codewords, flag IDs, or schema names in prose - gates expressed diegetically only
rules.rationale: Show the world, hide the gears

rules.statement: No seeds, models, or DAW notes visible in player-facing content
Two clear options beat five near-synonyms - choices must differ in intent AND outcome
rules.rationale: Players should feel real difference between choices

heuristics.context: Starting work on a section brief
heuristics.guidance: Check for existing sections with the same brief_ref before creating new

examples.situation: Assigned to write section for brief SB-042
examples.approach: search_workspace with query: 'type:section brief_ref:SB-042' to find existing

heuristics.context: Writing prose from a Plotwright brief
heuristics.guidance: Keep Plotwright's intent on screen, don't invent new topology, respect stated constraints

examples.situation: Brief specifies two choices leading to different outcomes
examples.approach: Write exactly two contrastive choices; don't add third option

heuristics.guidance: Verify gate reads as world fact (badge, knowledge, reputation, tool) and PN can enforce naturally

examples.good: The union office requires a member token to enter
examples.bad: This choice is locked until you obtain codeword:union_member

heuristics.guidance: Don't fight the register; propose alternatives if something feels wrong

examples.situation: Brief suggests phrasing that conflicts with style guide
examples.approach: Propose alternative to Style Lead rather than ignoring brief

steps: Search for existing section with same brief_ref
If found, update existing section; if not, create new
Set brief_ref to Section Brief ID (format: SB-NNN)
Verify brief_ref is set to Section Brief ID (SB-NNN)
Check all choices lead somewhere (anchor resolution)

definitions.meaning: A gate expressed as an in-world fact that PN can naturally enforce
distinguishing_features: Reads as world condition (needs badge, lacks knowledge)
No internal schema names or IDs

definitions.meaning: Choice options that differ meaningfully in both intent and outcome
Required field linking section to its source Section Brief
distinguishing_features: Must be set on every section artifact

warnings.consequence: Orphaned section; Gatekeeper can't find it; Showrunner can't track progress
warnings.prevention: Always set brief_ref when creating section

warnings.failure_mode: Creating duplicate section for same brief
warnings.consequence: Multiple sections reference same brief; ambiguity in which is canonical
warnings.prevention: Search for existing section with brief_ref before creating new

warnings.failure_mode: Exposing codewords or flag IDs in prose
warnings.consequence: Breaks immersion; exposes internal mechanics to players
warnings.prevention: Express all gates diegetically; verify no schema names appear

warnings.consequence: Players confused about difference; feels like fake choice
warnings.prevention: Ensure verb or object differs; imply different outcomes

warnings.consequence: Undermines Plotwright's structural intent; may break graph
warnings.prevention: Honor brief constraints; escalate topology concerns to Plotwright
